title: Send whatsapp to people who submitted a form using the phone column
excerpt: We are using Workforms to collect submissions, and would like to send an automated WhatsApp message to those who submitted the form telling them we’ll be in touch and other details - using the data they filled in the Pho…

posts.author: YaelC (Yael  Costi)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are using Workforms to collect submissions, and would like to send an automated WhatsApp message to those who submitted the form telling them we’ll be in touch and other details - using the data they filled in the Phone column.
This message should be sent upon submission from our WhatsApp business account.
We are open to integrating with subscription paid platforms (Twilio, Make, Zapier etc).

Anyone has some experience with this type of scenario? Are you familiar with the best way to do that?

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Yael!

It does sound like our Twilio integration would be a good fit for this! You could use a recipe like the following:

The “when a new item is created” trigger is important because the form will create a new item in the board it is connected to with each form that is submitted. So every time a form is submitted, the details will populate the board as a new item and trigger this automation recipe.

In the bolded “SMS” field, you’ll be able to craft your message and incorporate details from the data they submitted via the form.

I hope this is helpful! Let us know if you have any other questions - we are here to help!

posts.author: YaelC (Yael  Costi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Charlotte!
I saw this automation, and am unclear if “someone” would include the phone column of the item created?
Also - this automation is for SMS, does this also work with WhatsApp?

thanks again,
Yael

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @YaelC, I want to share Quick Message. It allows you to send pre-filled messages to contacts using the phone column via WhatsApp, Telegram, or iMessage. Check it out: https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000631/quick-message

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Krunchy,

We built an app that does exactly this. It’s called SuperSend. It allows you to communicate over whatsapp, imessage and telegram on monday.

We are constantly adding features.

You can check it out here: SuperSend - monday.com Marketplace

Or if you want an automation, you can use this app with this recipe to get a whatsapp link for any phone number:

Screenshot 2024-12-20 at 15.18.302632×1044 192 KB
